name: AI Workload Types and Use Cases
description: The major categories of AI workloads and how they map to real-world business problems solved by Azure AI services.
key_facts: Common AI workloads include computer vision, NLP, speech, decision-making, and generative AI
Anomaly detection identifies unusual patterns in data to flag issues like fraud or equipment failure
Knowledge mining extracts insights from large volumes of unstructured content
Conversational AI builds bots and virtual agents using language understanding
Responsible AI principles: fairness, reliability, privacy, inclusiveness, transparency, and accountability
common_misconceptions: AI workloads are not limited to prediction; they span vision, language, decision, and generative tasks
AI fairness is not automatic; models must be intentionally designed and tested to avoid bias

name: Responsible AI Principles
description: Microsoft's six guiding principles for developing and deploying AI systems ethically and responsibly.
key_facts: Fairness: AI systems should treat all people equitably without discrimination
Reliability and Safety: AI should perform reliably and safely, especially in high-stakes scenarios
Privacy and Security: AI must respect data privacy and be protected against attacks
Inclusiveness: AI benefits should be accessible to all people regardless of ability
Transparency: AI systems should be explainable so people understand how decisions are made
common_misconceptions: Transparency and explainability are not the same; transparency is about intent, explainability is about model behavior
Responsible AI is not just an ethical consideration—it is increasingly a legal and regulatory requirement
aws_services: Azure Machine Learning Responsible AI Dashboard

name: Machine Learning Fundamentals
description: Core concepts of machine learning including types of learning, training, and evaluation.
key_facts: Supervised learning uses labeled training data to predict outcomes; includes classification and regression
Unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data; includes clustering and dimensionality reduction
Reinforcement learning trains agents through reward and penalty feedback loops
Features are the input variables; labels are the known output values used in training
Overfitting occurs when a model memorizes training data and performs poorly on new data
common_misconceptions: Deep learning is not a separate category from machine learning; it is a subset of ML using neural networks
More training data is not always better; data quality matters as much as quantity

name: Azure Machine Learning Service
description: Microsoft's cloud platform for building, training, and deploying machine learning models at scale.
key_facts: Azure ML Studio provides a web-based IDE for authoring, training, and deploying ML models
Automated ML (AutoML) automatically selects algorithms and tunes hyperparameters for a given dataset
Designer provides a drag-and-drop interface for building ML pipelines without code
Azure ML supports popular frameworks including PyTorch, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn
Models are deployed as real-time endpoints or batch inference pipelines
common_misconceptions: AutoML does not eliminate the need for data preparation; garbage-in still produces garbage-out
Azure ML Studio (classic) is deprecated; the current service is simply Azure Machine Learning

name: Model Training and Evaluation Metrics
description: The process of training ML models and the metrics used to assess their performance.
key_facts: Training set is used to fit the model; validation set tunes hyperparameters; test set measures final performance
Accuracy measures percentage of correct predictions; useful for balanced datasets
Precision measures how many predicted positives are actually positive; Recall measures how many real positives are found
F1 Score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall; useful when class distribution is uneven
Regression uses RMSE and R-squared (R²) instead of classification metrics
common_misconceptions: High accuracy on imbalanced datasets can be misleading; a model that always predicts the majority class scores high
AUC-ROC above 0.5 means some predictive power; 1.0 is perfect; 0.5 is random guessing

name: Computer Vision Concepts and Azure Services
description: AI capabilities that enable machines to interpret and analyze visual content from images and video.
key_facts: Image classification assigns a label to an entire image (e.g., 'cat' or 'dog')
Object detection identifies and locates multiple objects within an image using bounding boxes
Semantic segmentation labels every pixel in an image with a class, not just bounding boxes
Azure AI Vision (formerly Computer Vision API) provides pre-built image analysis capabilities
Azure Custom Vision allows training custom image classification and object detection models without ML expertise
common_misconceptions: Image classification and object detection are different; detection locates multiple objects in an image
Custom Vision is not the same as Azure AI Vision; Custom Vision is for training domain-specific models

name: Natural Language Processing Fundamentals
description: AI techniques that enable machines to understand, interpret, and generate human language.
key_facts: Key phrase extraction identifies the main concepts in a piece of text
Sentiment analysis determines the emotional tone of text: positive, negative, or neutral
Named entity recognition (NER) identifies and classifies entities like people, places, and organizations
Language detection identifies the language in which text is written
Azure AI Language (formerly Text Analytics) provides all these NLP capabilities as a managed service
common_misconceptions: Sentiment analysis gives a score, not just a binary positive/negative; it returns confidence scores per class
NER and key phrase extraction are different; NER classifies entity types while key phrases are just important terms

name: Conversational AI and Azure Bot Service
description: AI systems that enable natural language conversations between humans and machines through chatbots and virtual agents.
key_facts: Azure Bot Service provides a platform for building, testing, and deploying conversational AI bots
Language Understanding (LUIS) enables bots to interpret user intent from natural language
Question Answering (QnA Maker successor in Azure AI Language) creates FAQ-style knowledge bases
Copilot Studio (formerly Power Virtual Agents) offers a no-code way to build bots
Multi-turn conversation supports context continuity across multiple exchanges
common_misconceptions: LUIS and QnA Maker serve different purposes; LUIS interprets intent while QnA answers structured FAQ questions
Azure Bot Service is not a standalone AI; it requires integration with language services for understanding

name: Azure AI Speech Services
description: Azure services that convert spoken audio to text and generate synthetic speech from text.
key_facts: Speech-to-Text (STT) transcribes audio into written text in real-time or batch mode
Text-to-Speech (TTS) converts text into natural-sounding synthesized speech
Speaker recognition identifies or verifies speakers based on voice characteristics
Speech translation transcribes and translates spoken language simultaneously
Azure AI Speech Studio provides a no-code interface to test and build speech scenarios
common_misconceptions: STT accuracy depends heavily on audio quality and can be improved with custom acoustic models
Neural TTS voices sound natural but are clearly disclosed as synthetic; they cannot impersonate real people

name: Generative AI Concepts and Azure OpenAI
description: AI models that generate new content including text, images, and code, and Azure services that provide access to them.
key_facts: Large Language Models (LLMs) are trained on massive text datasets and generate human-like text
Azure OpenAI Service provides access to GPT-4, DALL-E, and Embeddings models via REST APIs
Prompt engineering is the practice of crafting inputs to guide LLM outputs effectively
Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) combines LLMs with a knowledge base to reduce hallucinations
Tokens are the unit of text LLMs process; costs are typically billed per 1,000 tokens
common_misconceptions: Generative AI does not have true reasoning; it predicts statistically likely next tokens based on training data
Azure OpenAI is not the same as OpenAI's API; it runs in Azure with enterprise security and compliance

name: Azure AI Studio and Copilot Development
description: Microsoft's unified platform for building, evaluating, and deploying generative AI applications and copilots.
key_facts: Azure AI Studio provides a web-based environment for building generative AI apps with Azure OpenAI
The playground in AI Studio allows interactive testing of prompts against various foundation models
Grounding connects LLM responses to specific data sources using RAG patterns
Azure AI Studio supports deploying copilots to web apps, Teams, and custom applications
Safety filters in Azure OpenAI detect and block harmful content categories
common_misconceptions: Azure AI Studio is not the same as Azure Machine Learning Studio; it focuses specifically on generative AI
Grounding reduces but does not eliminate hallucinations; LLMs can still generate inaccurate claims

name: Azure AI Services (Cognitive Services)
description: A collection of pre-built AI capabilities available as REST APIs without requiring ML expertise.
key_facts: Azure AI Services is the umbrella brand for pre-built AI APIs formerly known as Cognitive Services
Categories include Vision, Speech, Language, Decision, and OpenAI
Services are available as multi-service resources or individual service resources
Each service uses an API key and endpoint for authentication
Free tier is available for most services with rate limits
common_misconceptions: Azure AI Services require no training data for pre-built models; custom models need labeled data
A multi-service AI resource uses one key for multiple services but is billed per service used

name: Clustering and Anomaly Detection
description: Unsupervised machine learning techniques for grouping similar data points and identifying outliers.
key_facts: Clustering groups data into clusters based on similarity without predefined labels
K-means clustering assigns each data point to one of K clusters by minimizing within-cluster variance
Anomaly detection identifies data points that deviate significantly from expected patterns
Azure Anomaly Detector API detects anomalies in time-series data automatically
Clustering is unsupervised; anomaly detection can be supervised or unsupervised
common_misconceptions: Clustering does not tell you what clusters mean; humans must interpret the groups
Anomaly detection does not require labeled anomaly examples in the unsupervised approach

name: Azure AI Document Intelligence
description: AI service that extracts text, key-value pairs, tables, and structures from documents using machine learning.
key_facts: Azure AI Document Intelligence (formerly Form Recognizer) extracts structured data from forms and documents
Pre-built models support invoices, receipts, IDs, business cards, and tax forms without training
Custom models can be trained on domain-specific documents using labeled samples
Optical Character Recognition (OCR) is the foundational technology for reading text from images
Results include extracted text, confidence scores, and bounding box coordinates
common_misconceptions: OCR and Document Intelligence are not the same; OCR reads text, Document Intelligence understands structure
Pre-built models cover common document types; less common formats require custom model training

name: Azure AI Search and Knowledge Mining
description: AI-powered search service that indexes and retrieves information from diverse data sources using cognitive skills.
key_facts: Azure AI Search (formerly Cognitive Search) provides full-text search with AI enrichment
Indexers crawl data sources and populate search indexes automatically
Skillsets apply cognitive skills (OCR, sentiment, NER) to enrich content during indexing
Semantic ranking uses language models to re-rank results based on meaning, not just keywords
Azure AI Search is the retrieval component in RAG architectures for generative AI applications
common_misconceptions: Azure AI Search is not just a text search engine; it uses AI to understand and enrich content
Knowledge Store persists AI-enriched data to Azure Storage for analysis, separate from the search index
